danger_signals.visual_form: Palace guards, Iago's cousins, environmental hazards (pots, rocks, fish)
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to Aladdin's health; collision causes damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual indicator on HUD (top-left corner) that shortens progressively
danger_signals.meaning: Health depletion; longer trail = safer, shorter trail = critical danger
Inverted signal: appears as item but represents Jafar's threat; triggering it eliminates all on-screen enemies, suggesting Jafar's power as antagonist

danger_signals.signal: Jafar imagery in Genie's Bonus Machine
danger_signals.visual_form: Picture of Jafar among reward tiles
danger_signals.meaning: Landing on Jafar ends the bonus minigame; visual representation of the primary antagonist as game-ending threat

safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration; collecting restores Aladdin's health (extends smoke trail)
Safe revival points; passing through grants continued play after losing a life

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible icons hidden in levels shaped like Aladdin's head
safety_signals.meaning: Extra lives; provides safety buffer against game over

safety_signals.visual_form: Minigame environment with reward tiles and Genie imagery
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity for resource acquisition (gems, apples, lives); progression reward for collecting Genie Tokens

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Golden head icon (represents Aladdin); Genie's lamp smoke trail (associated with Aladdin's status)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scimitar as signature weapon; apples as ammunition (thematic to street urchin background)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue (Genie Hearts are blue; Genie's lamp smoke indicator is blue-associated)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Genie's lamp; Genie Tokens; Genie's Bonus Machine imagery
Jafar's lamp (smart bomb item); Jafar portrait in Genie's Bonus Machine
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jafar's lamp appears as collectible but represents his power; landing on Jafar's picture ends bonus rounds
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Palace guards (Jafar's forces); represented as primary threat in minigame conclusion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Abu Token (collectible that unlocks bonus levels)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bonus levels feature Abu as player character; separate hazard set (rocks, pots, Iago's cousins) reflects different context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard platformer environment with palace/street settings from film; enemies actively patrol or attack; collectibles scattered naturally
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aladdin moves left-right, engages enemies with scimitar or apples; guards pursue; standard hazard avoidance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to Genie's Bonus Machine environment; visual shift to minigame interface with reward tiles and Genie imagery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player shifts from combat/platforming to luck-based minigame; button-pressing interaction replaces movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smoke trail from Genie's lamp visibly shortened on HUD; visual warning of imminent game-over state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must prioritize finding blue Genie Hearts; heightened threat perception from enemies and hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental shift to Abu-specific level design; visual presentation of falling collectibles and different hazard types (pots, rocks, fish, Iago's cousins)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Abu character replaces Aladdin; gameplay shifts from combat to dodging falling hazards while collecting resources

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Jafar's lamp (smart bomb) collected and triggered
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual elimination effect of all on-screen enemies; Jafar's power manifested as weapon against his own minions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All enemies disappear momentarily; temporary reprieve from threat; Jafar's antagonistic presence inverted into player advantage

environmental_storytelling.example: Aladdin uses a scimitar (close-range) and apples (long-range ammunition), thematically tied to street urchin background and resourcefulness rather than royal weaponry
Abu's bonus levels feature different hazards (pots, rocks, fish) compared to Aladdin's levels, showing how the same palace/environment is experienced differently by different characters

Genie Hearts and Genie's lamp imagery throughout levels reinforce Genie as Aladdin's magical ally; Jafar's lamp as smart bomb inverts his threat into a tool, reflecting his status as antagonist but also his magical power
Vases as checkpoint markers integrate Islamic/Arabian architectural elements into the mechanical systems, grounding game progression in world setting
Palace guards appear as recurring enemy type, visually reinforcing the political/social conflict of the film narrative (street urchin vs. palace authority)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Pursue Aladdin when detected; represent Jafar's authority and institutional threat
Attack in both main levels and Abu's bonus levels; represent Jafar's minions across contexts
In Abu's bonus level, fall from above; in main levels, present static obstacles; world-state dependent threat presentation
Smoke trail shortens with damage, lengthens with healing; continuously communicates player vulnerability state
When collected and triggered, eliminates all on-screen enemies; Jafar's power weaponized against his own minions, reflecting antagonist role

notes: Disney's Aladdin uses visual communication primarily through health/status indicators (Genie lamp smoke trail), faction representation (Genie Hearts = ally; palace guards = Jafar's authority), and item iconography (Jafar's lamp inverts his threat into a tool). The game relies on film-faithful animation and character recognition rather than complex world-state visualization. Checkpoint vases and bonus machine access communicate progression safety. Enemy types (guards, Iago's cousins) and hazard variation between Aladdin and Abu levels show how the same world is experienced differently by different entities. The visual language is primarily reward-based (blue = safe/healing; gold = bonus; Jafar's portrait = danger) rather than complex environmental storytelling.
